Entry<T> | undefined = {\n\t\t\tselector,\n\t\t\tprovider,\n\t\t\t_score: -1,\n\t\t\t_time: this._clock++\n\t\t};\n\n\t\tthis._entries.push(entry);\n\t\tthis._lastCandidate = undefined;\n\t\tthis._onDidChange.fire(this._entries.length);\n\n\t\treturn toDisposable(() => {\n\t\t\tif (entry) {\n\t\t\t\tconst idx = this._entries.indexOf(entry);\n\t\t\t\tif (idx >= 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\tthis._entries.splice(idx, 1);\n\t\t\t\t\tthis._lastCandidate = undefined;\n\t\t\t\t\tthis._onDidChange.fire(this._entries.length);\n\t\t\t\t\tentry = undefined;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t});\n\t}\n\n\thas(model: ITextModel): boolean {\n\t\treturn this.all(model).length > 0;\n\t}\n\n\tall(model: ITextModel): T[] {\n\t\tif (!model) {\n\t\t\treturn (this._lastCandidate?.equals(candidate)) {\n\t\t\t// nothing has changed\n\t\t\treturn;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tthis._lastCandidate = candidate;\n\n\t\tfor (const entry of this._entries) {\n\t\t\tentry._score = {\n\t\t\t\tif (recursive) {\n\t\t\t\t\tentry._score = 0;\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t// support for one exclusive selector that overwrites\n\t\t\t\t\t// any other selector\n\t\t\t\t\tfor (const entry of this._entries) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tentry._score = 0;\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tentry._score = 1000;\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// needs sorting\n\t\tthis._entries.sort(LanguageFeatureRegistry._compareByScoreAndTime);\n\t}\n\n\tprivate static _compareByScoreAndTime(a: Entry<any>, b: Entry<any>): number {\n\t\tif (a._score < b._score) {\n\t\t\treturn -1;\n\t\t} else if (a._score > b._score) {\n\t\t\treturn 1;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// De-prioritize built-in providers\n\t\tif (isBuiltinSelector(a.selector) && !isBuiltinSelector(b.selector)) {\n\t\t\treturn 1;\n\t\t} else if (!isBuiltinSelector(a.selector) && isBuiltinSelector(b.selector)) {\n\t\t\treturn -1;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif (a._time < b._time) {\n\t\t\treturn 1;\n\t\t} else if (a._time > b._time) {\n\t\t\treturn -1;\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn 0;\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunction isBuiltinSelector(selector: LanguageSelector): boolean {\n\tif (typeof selector === 'string') {\n\t\treturn false;\n\t}\n\n\tif (Array.isArray(selector)) {\n\t\treturn selector.some(isBuiltinSelector);\n\t}\n\n\treturn Boolean((selector as LanguageFilter).isBuiltin);\n}\n\n","numLines":230,"startLine":1,"totalLines":230}}}

null);\n\t}\n}\n\nexport function isMultilineRegexSource(searchString: string): boolean {\n\tif (!searchString || searchString.length === 0) {\n\t\treturn false;\n\t}\n\n\tfor (let i = 0, len = number[];\n\n\tconstructor(text: string) {\n\t\tconst lineFeedsOffsets: number[] = [];\n\t\tlet lineFeedsOffsetsLen = 0;\n\t\tfor (let i = 0, textLen = text.length; i < textLen; i++) {\n\t\t\tif (text.charCodeAt(i) === CharCode.LineFeed) {\n\t\t\t\tlineFeedsOffsets[lineFeedsOffsetsLen++] = i;\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tthis._lineFeedsOffsets = lineFeedsOffsets;\n\t}\n\n\tpublic + ((max - min) / 2 >> 0);\n\n\t\t\tif (lineFeedsOffsets[mid] >= offset) {\n\t\t\t\tmax = mid - 1;\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif (lineFeedsOffsets[mid + 1] >= offset) {\n\t\t\t\t\t// bingo!\n\t\t\t\t\tmin = mid;\n\t\t\t\t\tmax = mid;\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tmin = mid + 1;\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn min + 1;\n\t}\n}\n\nexport class TextModelSearch {\n\n\tpublic static findMatches(model: TextModel, searchParams: SearchParams, searchRange: Range, captureMatches: boolean, limitResultCount: number): FindMatch[] {\n\t\tconst searchData = searchParams.parseSearchRequest();\n\t\tif (!searchData) {\n\t\t\treturn [];\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif (searchData.regex.multiline) {\n\t\t\treturn this._doFindMatchesMultiline(model, searchRange, new matchIndex + lineFeedCountBeforeMatch /* add as many \\r as there were \\n */;\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tstartOffset = deltaOffset + matchIndex;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tlet endOffset: number;\n\t\tif (lfCounter) {\n\t\t\tconst lineFeedCountBeforeEndOfMatch = lfCounter.findLineFeedCountBeforeOffset(matchIndex + match0.length);\n\t\t\tconst lineFeedCountInMatch = lineFeedCountBeforeEndOfMatch - lineFeedCountBeforeMatch;\n\t\t\tendOffset = startOffset + match0.length + lineFeedCountInMatch /* add as many \\r as there were \\n */;\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tendOffset = startOffset + match0.length;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tconst startPosition = model.getPositionAt(startOffset);\n\t\tconst endPosition = model.getPositionAt(endOffset);\n\t\treturn new Range(startPosition.lineNumber, startPosition.column, endPosition.lineNumber, endPosition.column);\n\t}\n\n\tprivate static _doFindMatchesMultiline(model: TextModel, searchRange: Range, searcher: Searcher, captureMatches: boolean, limitResultCount: number): FindMatch[] {\n\t\tconst deltaOffset = model.getOffsetAt(searchRange.getStartPosition());\n\t\t// We always execute multiline search over the lines joined with \\n\n\t\t// EndOfLinePreference.LF);\n\t\tconst lfCounter = (model.getEOL() === '\\r\\n' ? new LineFeedCounter(text) : null);\n\n\t\tconst result: FindMatch[] = [];\n\t\tlet counter = 0;\n\n\t\tlet m: RegExpExecArray | null;\n\t\tsearcher.reset(0);\n\t\twhile ((m = searcher.next(text))) {\n\t\t\tresult[counter++] = createFindMatch(this._getMultilineMatchRange(model, deltaOffset, text, lfCounter, TextModel, searchRange: Range, searchData: SearchData, captureMatches: boolean, limitResultCount: number): FindMatch[] {\n\t\tconst result: FindMatch[] = [];\n\t\tlet resultLen = 0;\n\n\t\t// Early model.getLineContent(searchRange.startLineNumber).substring(searchRange.startColumn - 1, searchRange.endColumn - 1);\n\t\t\tresultLen = this._findMatchesInLine(searchData, text, searchRange.startLineNumber, searchRange.startColumn - 1, resultLen, result, captureMatches, limitResultCount);\n\t\t\treturn result;\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Collect results from first line\n\t\tconst text = model.getLineContent(searchRange.startLineNumber).substring(searchRange.startColumn - 1);\n\t\tresultLen = this._findMatchesInLine(searchData, text, searchRange.startLineNumber, searchRange.startColumn - 1, resultLen, result, captureMatches, limitResultCount);\n\n\t\t// Collect results from middle lines\n\t\tfor (let lineNumber = searchRange.startLineNumber + 1; lineNumber < searchRange.endLineNumber && resultLen < limitResultCount; lineNumber++) {\n\t\t\tresultLen = this._findMatchesInLine(searchData, model.getLineContent(lineNumber), lineNumber, 0, resultLen, result, captureMatches, limitResultCount);\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Collect results from last line\n\t\tif (resultLen < limitResultCount) {\n\t\t\tconst text = Makes the string safe from\n * being used e.g. in HTMLElement.innerHTML.\n */\nexport function escape(html: string): string {\n\treturn html.replace(/[<>&]/g, function (match) {\n\t\tswitch (match) {\n\t\t\tcase '<': return '&lt;';\n\t\t\tcase '>': return '&gt;';\n\t\t\tcase '&': return '&amp;';\n\t\t\tdefault: return match;\n\t\t}\n\t});\n}\n\n/**\n * Escapes regular expression characters in a given string\n */\nexport function escapeRegExpCharacters(value: string): string {\n\treturn value.replace(/[\\\\\\{\\}\\*\\+\\?\\|\\^\\$\\.\\[\\]\\(\\)]/g, '\\\\$&');\n}\n\n/**\n * Counts how often `substr` occurs inside `value`.\n */\nexport function count(value: string, substr: string): number {\n\tlet result = 0;\n\tlet index = value.indexOf(substr);\n\twhile (index !== -1) {\n\t\tresult++;\n\t\tindex = value.indexOf(substr, index + substr.length);","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1319}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01BSYFDkQ7b89D6CgqaF5U3T","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Perfect! I've now confirmed all the bugs. Let me analyze each one systematically:\n\n1. **Position.ts line 103** - `isBefore` method: The comparison uses `a.column <= b.column` when it should be `a.column < b.column`. According to the docstring: \"If the two positions are equal, the result will be false.\" But with `<=`, equal positions would return true.\n\n2. **Range.ts line 97** - `containsPosition` method: The check uses `position.column >= range.endColumn` to return false, but the docstring says \"If the position is at the edges, will return true.\" If position.column == endColumn (the edge), this should return true, not false. Should be `>` not `>=`.\n\n3. **strings.ts line 226-227** - `createRegExp` function: The 'i' flag means case-insensitive. When `options.matchCase` is true, we want case-SENSITIVE matching, so we should NOT add 'i'. When false, we want case-INSENSITIVE, so we SHOULD add 'i'. This is completely inverted!\n\n4. **textModelSearch.ts line 489** - `isValidMatch` function: For whole-word matching, BOTH left AND right boundaries must be word boundaries. Currently using OR (||) which accepts matches where only one side is a boundary. Should be AND (&&).\n\n5. **languageFeatureRegistry.ts line 195-199** - `_compareByScoreAndTime` function: Returns -1 when `a._score < b._score`, meaning lower scores come first in the sorted array. But higher scores should have higher priority and come first. The comparison is backwards - should return 1 when a < b, or invert the comparison operators.\n\nLet me create the fixes now.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":10411,"cache_read_input_tokens":44786,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":10411},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ebd21f83-bb77-4828-9b67-6b102d8bebe6","uuid":"813f1203-dd69-4b9a-8e42-005ce6fbdd11"}

According to the docstring, equal positions should return false, but currently returns true when positions are equal. This breaks position ordering contracts used throughout the editor (cursors, selections, search ranges).\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/src/vs/editor/common/core/position.ts\\n+++ b/src/vs/editor/common/core/position.ts\\n@@ -100,7 +100,7 @@\\n \\t\\tif (b.lineNumber < a.lineNumber) {\\n \\t\\t\\treturn false;\\n \\t\\t}\\n-\\t\\treturn a.column <= b.column;\\n+\\t\\treturn a.column < b.column;\\n \\t}\\n \\n \\t/**\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"src/vs/editor/common/core/range.ts\",\n    \"line\": 97,\n    \"severity\": \"critical\",\n    \"description\": \"The containsPosition() method uses '>=' instead of '>' when checking if a position is at the end edge of a range. The docstring explicitly states 'If the position is at the edges, will return true', but the current check excludes positions at the end column. This breaks containment semantics for decorations, find-and-replace highlighting, and bracket matching that rely on inclusive edge behavior.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/src/vs/editor/common/core/range.ts\\n+++ b/src/vs/editor/common/core/range.ts\\n@@ -94,7 +94,7 @@\\n \\t\\tif (position.lineNumber === range.startLineNumber && position.column < range.startColumn) {\\n \\t\\t\\treturn false;\\n \\t\\t}\\n-\\t\\tif (position.lineNumber === range.endLineNumber && position.column >= range.endColumn) {\\n+\\t\\tif (position.lineNumber === range.endLineNumber && position.column > range.endColumn) {\\n \\t\\t\\treturn false;\\n \\t\\t}\\n \\t\\treturn true;\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"src/vs/base/common/strings.ts\",\n    \"line\": 226,\n    \"severity\": \"critical\",\n    \"description\": \"The matchCase flag logic is inverted. The 'i' RegExp flag means case-INsensitive matching. When options.matchCase is true, we need case-SENSITIVE matching (no 'i' flag). When false, we need case-INSENSITIVE matching ('i' flag). The current code adds 'i' when matchCase is true, which is backwards. This breaks all case-sensitive/insensitive find operations, affecting textModelSearch.ts which calls createRegExp().\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/src/vs/base/common/strings.ts\\n+++ b/src/vs/base/common/strings.ts\\n@@ -223,7 +223,7 @@\\n \\tif (options.global) {\\n \\t\\tmodifiers += 'g';\\n \\t}\\n-\\tif (options.matchCase) {\\n+\\tif (!options.matchCase) {\\n \\t\\tmodifiers += 'i';\\n \\t}\\n \\tif (options.multiline) {\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"src/vs/editor/common/model/textModelSearch.ts\",\n    \"line\": 489,\n    \"severity\": \"critical\",\n    \"description\": \"The isValidMatch() function uses OR (||) instead of AND (&&) when checking word boundaries. For whole-word search, BOTH the left AND right boundaries must be word boundaries. Using OR accepts matches where only one side is a boundary (e.g., matching 'test' in 'test123' with whole-word mode enabled). This breaks whole-word find operations and affects any feature using word-boundary matching.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/src/vs/editor/common/model/textModelSearch.ts\\n+++ b/src/vs/editor/common/model/textModelSearch.ts\\n@@ -486,7 +486,7 @@\\n \\n export function isValidMatch(wordSeparators: WordCharacterClassifier, text: string, textLength: number, matchStartIndex: number, matchLength: number): boolean {\\n \\treturn (\\n-\\t\\tleftIsWordBounday(wordSeparators, text, textLength, matchStartIndex, matchLength)\\n-\\t\\t|| rightIsWordBounday(wordSeparators, text, textLength, matchStartIndex, matchLength)\\n+\\t\\tleftIsWordBounday(wordSeparators, text, textLength, matchStartIndex, matchLength)\\n+\\t\\t&& rightIsWordBounday(wordSeparators, text, textLength, matchStartIndex, matchLength)\\n \\t);\\n }\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"src/vs/editor/common/languageFeatureRegistry.ts\",\n    \"line\": 195,\n    \"severity\": \"critical\",\n    \"description\": \"The score comparison in _compareByScoreAndTime() is inverted. When a._score < b._score (lower score), it returns -1, causing lower scores to appear first in the sorted array. However, higher scores should indicate higher priority/relevance and must appear first for proper provider ordering. The time comparison correctly prioritizes newer registrations (returns 1 for lower time), but score comparison is backwards, causing providers with worse matches to be prioritized over better matches.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/src/vs/editor/common/languageFeatureRegistry.ts\\n+++ b/src/vs/editor/common/languageFeatureRegistry.ts\\n@@ -192,10 +192,10 @@\\n \\tprivate static _compareByScoreAndTime(a: Entry<any>, b: Entry<any>): number {\\n \\t\\tif (a._score < b._score) {\\n-\\t\\t\\treturn -1;\\n+\\t\\t\\treturn 1;\\n \\t\\t} else if (a._score > b._score) {\\n-\\t\\t\\treturn 1;\\n+\\t\\t\\treturn -1;\\n \\t\\t}\\n \\n \\t\\t// De-prioritize built-in providers\\n\"\n  }\n]\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":10411,"cache_read_input_tokens":44786,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":10411},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ebd21f83-bb77-4828-9b67-6b102d8bebe6","uuid":"9b15af66-631e-483e-bec4-7cc6885ce5d0"}
